info.title: Bitcoin Research Kit
info.description:
API for querying Bitcoin blockchain data, mempool state, and on-chain series.

### Features

- **[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible**: Blocks, transactions, addresses, mining, fees, and mempool endpoints match the mempool.space REST API
- **Series**: Thousands of on-chain time-series across multiple indexes (date, block height, etc.)
- **Multiple formats**: JSON and CSV output
- **LLM-optimized**: [`/llms.txt`](/llms.txt) for discovery, [`/api.json`](/api.json) compact OpenAPI spec for tool use (full spec at [`/openapi.json`](/openapi.json))

### Quick start

```bash
curl -s https://bitview.space/api/blocks/tip/height
curl -s https://bitview.space/api/v1/fees/recommended
curl -s https://bitview.space/api/mempool
curl -s https://bitview.space/api/series/search?q=price
```

### Errors

All errors return structured JSON with a consistent format:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "not_found",
    "code": "series_not_found",
    "message": "'foo' not found, did you mean 'bar'?",
    "doc_url": "/api"
  }
}
```

- **`type`**: Error category — `invalid_request` (400), `forbidden` (403), `not_found` (404), `unavailable` (503), or `internal` (500)
- **`code`**: Machine-readable error code (e.g. `invalid_address`, `series_not_found`, `weight_exceeded`)
- **`message`**: Human-readable description
- **`doc_url`**: Link to API documentation

### Client Libraries

- [JavaScript](https://www.npmjs.com/package/brk-client)
- [Python](https://pypi.org/project/brk-client/)
- [Rust](https://crates.io/crates/brk_client)

### Links

- [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoinresearchkit/brk)
- [Bitview](https://bitview.space) - Web app built on this API

get.description: Liveness probe. Returns server identity, uptime, and indexed/computed heights from local state only (no bitcoind round-trip). For real chain-tip catch-up, see `/api/server/sync`.
Returns the current version of the API server

get.description:
URPD for the most recent available date in the cohort. The response's `date` field echoes which date was served.

See the URPD tag description for the response shape and `agg` options.

parameters.name: agg
parameters.description: Aggregation strategy. Default: raw (no aggregation). Accepts `bucket` as alias.

schema.description: Aggregation strategy. Default: raw (no aggregation). Accepts `bucket` as alias.

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
URPD for a (cohort, date) pair. Returns `{ cohort, date, aggregation, close, total_supply, buckets }` where each bucket is `{ price_floor, supply, realized_cap, unrealized_pnl }`.

See the URPD tag description for unit conventions and `agg` options.

parameters.name: agg
parameters.description: Aggregation strategy. Default: raw (no aggregation). Accepts `bucket` as alias.

schema.description: Aggregation strategy. Default: raw (no aggregation). Accepts `bucket` as alias.

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-price)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Get historical BTC/USD price. Optionally specify a UNIX timestamp to get the price at that time.

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-historical-price)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Retrieve address information including balance and transaction counts. Supports all standard Bitcoin address types (P2PKH, P2SH, P2WPKH, P2WSH, P2TR).

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Get transaction history for an address, newest first. Returns up to 50 mempool transactions plus a confirmed page sized to fill the response to 50 total (chain floor of 25, so 25-50 confirmed depending on mempool weight). To paginate further confirmed history, use `/address/{address}/txs/chain/{last_seen_txid}`.

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address-transactions)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Get the first 25 confirmed transactions for an address. For pagination, use the path-style form `/txs/chain/{last_seen_txid}`.

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address-transactions-chain)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Get the next 25 confirmed transactions strictly older than `after_txid` (Esplora-canonical pagination form, matches mempool.space).

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address-transactions-chain)*

parameters.name: after_txid
parameters.description: Last txid from the previous page (return transactions strictly older than this)

schema.description: Last txid from the previous page (return transactions strictly older than this)

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Get unconfirmed transactions for an address from the mempool, newest first (up to 50).

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address-transactions-mempool)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Get unspent transaction outputs (UTXOs) for an address. Returns txid, vout, value, and confirmation status for each UTXO.

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address-utxo)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Validate a Bitcoin address and get information about its type and scriptPubKey. Returns `isvalid: false` with an error message for invalid addresses.

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-address-validate)*

get.description: Returns an opaque hash that changes whenever the projected next block changes. Same value as the mempool ETag. Useful as a freshness/liveness signal: if it stays constant for tens of seconds on a live network, the mempool sync loop has stalled.

get.description:
Returns up to 25 most-recent RBF replacement trees across the whole mempool. Each entry has the same shape as `tx_rbf().replacements`.

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description:
Like `/api/v1/replacements`, but limited to trees where at least one predecessor was non-signaling (full-RBF).

*[Mempool.space docs](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest#get-fullrbf-replacements)*

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description: Bitcoin Core's `getblocktemplate` selection: full transaction bodies in GBT order with aggregate stats. The returned `hash` is an opaque content token; pass it as `<hash>` on `/api/v1/mempool/block-template/diff/{hash}` to fetch deltas instead of refetching the whole template.

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description: Delta of the projected next block since `<hash>`. `order` is the full new template in order: each entry is either a number (index into the prior template the client cached at `<hash>`) or a transaction object (new body to insert at this position). Walk `order` once to rebuild; `removed` is a convenience list of txids that left so clients can evict cached bodies. After applying, use the response `hash` as `<hash>` on the next call to keep iterating. Returns `404` when `<hash>` has aged out of server history; clients should fall back to `/api/v1/mempool/block-template`.

304.description: Not modified - content unchanged since last request

get.description: Returns the current BTC/USD price in dollars, derived from on-chain round-dollar output patterns in the last 12 blocks plus mempool.

get.description: Full OpenAPI 3.1 specification for this API.
Compact OpenAPI specification optimized for LLM consumption. Removes redundant fields while preserving essential API information. Full spec available at `/openapi.json`.

indexed_height.description: Height of the last indexed block

computed_height.description: Height of the last computed block (series)

tip_height.description: Height of the chain tip (from Bitcoin node)

blocks_behind.description: Number of blocks behind the tip

last_indexed_at.description: Human-readable timestamp of the last indexed block (ISO 8601)

last_indexed_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp of the last indexed block

indexed_height.description: Height of the last indexed block

computed_height.description: Height of the last computed block (series)

tip_height.description: Height of the chain tip (from Bitcoin node)

blocks_behind.description: Number of blocks behind the tip

last_indexed_at.description: Human-readable timestamp of the last indexed block (ISO 8601)

last_indexed_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp of the last indexed block

DiskUsage.description: Disk usage of the indexed data

bitcoin_bytes.description: Bitcoin blocks directory size in bytes

ratio.description: brk as percentage of Bitcoin data

TreeNode.description: Hierarchical tree node for organizing series into categories

anyOf.description: Leaf node containing series metadata with schema

SeriesLeafWithSchema.description: SeriesLeaf with JSON Schema for client generation

Index.description:
Aggregation dimension for querying series. Includes time-based (date, week, month, year),
block-based (height, tx_index), and address/output type indexes.

SeriesCount.description: Series count statistics - distinct series and total series-index combinations

distinct_series.description: Number of unique series available (e.g., realized_price, market_cap)

total_endpoints.description: Total number of series-index combinations across all timeframes

lazy_endpoints.description: Number of lazy (computed on-the-fly) series-index combinations

stored_endpoints.description: Number of eager (stored on disk) series-index combinations

IndexInfo.description: Information about an available index and its query aliases

Pagination.description: Pagination parameters for paginated API endpoints

PaginatedSeries.description: A paginated list of available series names (1000 per page)

has_more.description: Whether more pages are available after the current one

Limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 100 if not specified.

DataRangeFormat.description: Range parameters with output format for API query parameters.

start.description: Inclusive start: integer index, date (YYYY-MM-DD), or timestamp (ISO 8601). Negative integers count from end. Aliases: `from`, `f`, `s`

end.description: Exclusive end: integer index, date (YYYY-MM-DD), or timestamp (ISO 8601). Negative integers count from end. Aliases: `to`, `t`, `e`

limit.description: Maximum number of values to return (ignored if `end` is set). Aliases: `count`, `c`, `l`

RangeIndex.description: A range boundary: integer index, date, or timestamp.

Date.description: Date in YYYYMMDD format stored as u32

SeriesData.description:
Series data with range information.

All series data endpoints return this structure when format is JSON.
This type is not instantiated - use `SeriesData::serialize()` to write JSON bytes directly.

index.description: The index type used for this query

start.description: Start index (inclusive) of the returned range

end.description: End index (exclusive) of the returned range

stamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the response was generated

Version.description:
Version tracking for data schema and computed values.

Used to detect when stored data needs to be recomputed due to changes
in computation logic or source data versions. Supports validation
against persisted versions to ensure compatibility.

start.description: Inclusive start: integer index, date (YYYY-MM-DD), or timestamp (ISO 8601). Negative integers count from end. Aliases: `from`, `f`, `s`

end.description: Exclusive end: integer index, date (YYYY-MM-DD), or timestamp (ISO 8601). Negative integers count from end. Aliases: `to`, `t`, `e`

limit.description: Maximum number of values to return (ignored if `end` is set). Aliases: `count`, `c`, `l`

Cohort.description:
URPD cohort identifier. Use `GET /api/urpd` to list available cohorts.

Validated at construction: non-empty, ASCII `[a-z0-9_]+`. Matches the
schemars enum value set; the type therefore proves "this is a valid
cohort name" wherever a `Cohort` is held.

UrpdAggregation.description:
Aggregation strategy for URPD buckets.
Options: raw (no aggregation), lin200/lin500/lin1000 (linear $200/$500/$1000),
log10/log50/log100/log200 (logarithmic with 10/50/100/200 buckets per decade).

CostBasisValue.description: Value type for the deprecated cost-basis distribution output.

UrpdCohortParam.description: Path parameters for per-cohort URPD endpoints.

agg.description: Aggregation strategy. Default: raw (no aggregation). Accepts `bucket` as alias.

Urpd.description:
UTXO Realized Price Distribution for a cohort on a specific date.

Supply is grouped by the close price at which each UTXO was last moved.
Each bucket exposes three values: supply in BTC, realized cap contribution
in USD (sum of `realized_price * supply` over the coins in the bucket), and
unrealized P&L in USD (`close * supply - realized_cap`, can be negative).

aggregation.description: Aggregation strategy applied to the buckets.

close.description: Close price on `date`, in USD. Anchor for `unrealized_pnl`.

total_supply.description: Sum of `supply` across all buckets, in BTC.

Bitcoin.description: Bitcoin amount as floating point (1 BTC = 100,000,000 satoshis)

UrpdBucket.description: A single bucket in a URPD snapshot.

price_floor.description: Lower bound of the bucket, in USD. Equals the exact realized price for `Raw`.

supply.description: Supply held with a last-move price inside this bucket, in BTC.

realized_cap.description: Realized cap contribution in USD: sum of `realized_price * supply` over the coins in this bucket.

unrealized_pnl.description: Unrealized P&L in USD against the close on the snapshot date: `close * supply - realized_cap`. Can be negative.

DetailedSeriesCount.description: Detailed series count with per-database breakdown

distinct_series.description: Number of unique series available (e.g., realized_price, market_cap)

total_endpoints.description: Total number of series-index combinations across all timeframes

lazy_endpoints.description: Number of lazy (computed on-the-fly) series-index combinations

stored_endpoints.description: Number of eager (stored on disk) series-index combinations

start.description: Inclusive start: integer index, date (YYYY-MM-DD), or timestamp (ISO 8601). Negative integers count from end. Aliases: `from`, `f`, `s`

end.description: Exclusive end: integer index, date (YYYY-MM-DD), or timestamp (ISO 8601). Negative integers count from end. Aliases: `to`, `t`, `e`

limit.description: Maximum number of values to return (ignored if `end` is set). Aliases: `count`, `c`, `l`

difficultyChange.description: Estimated difficulty change at next retarget (%)

timeAvg.description: Average block time in current epoch (milliseconds)

expectedBlocks.description: Expected blocks based on wall clock time since epoch start

Prices.description: Current price response matching mempool.space /api/v1/prices format

ExchangeRates.description: Exchange rates (USD base, on-chain only — no fiat pairs available)

AddrStats.description: Address information compatible with mempool.space API format

addr_type.description: Address type (p2pkh, p2sh, v0_p2wpkh, v0_p2wsh, v1_p2tr, etc.)

chain_stats.description: Statistics for confirmed transactions on the blockchain

mempool_stats.description: Statistics for unconfirmed transactions in the mempool

AddrChainStats.description:
Address statistics on the blockchain (confirmed transactions only)

Based on mempool.space's format with type_index extension.

funded_txo_count.description: Total number of transaction outputs that funded this address

funded_txo_sum.description: Total amount in satoshis received by this address across all funded outputs

spent_txo_count.description: Total number of transaction outputs spent from this address

spent_txo_sum.description: Total amount in satoshis spent from this address

tx_count.description: Total number of confirmed transactions involving this address

type_index.description: Index of this address within its type on the blockchain

realized_price.description: Realized price (average cost basis) in USD

TypeIndex.description: Index within its type (e.g., 0 for first P2WPKH address)

AddrMempoolStats.description:
Address statistics in the mempool (unconfirmed transactions only)

Based on mempool.space's format.

funded_txo_count.description: Number of unconfirmed transaction outputs funding this address

funded_txo_sum.description: Total amount in satoshis being received in unconfirmed transactions

spent_txo_count.description: Number of unconfirmed transaction inputs spending from this address

spent_txo_sum.description: Total amount in satoshis being spent in unconfirmed transactions

tx_count.description: Number of unconfirmed transactions involving this address

Transaction.description: Transaction information compatible with mempool.space API format

index.description: Internal transaction index (brk-specific, not in mempool.space)

version.description: Transaction version (raw i32 from Bitcoin protocol, may contain non-standard values in coinbase txs)

TxIndex.description:
Chain-wide transaction index (0 = the genesis coinbase). For an
in-block position, use `BlockTxIndex` instead.

TxVersionRaw.description:
Raw transaction version (i32) from Bitcoin protocol.
Unlike TxVersion (u8, indexed), this preserves non-standard values
used in coinbase txs for miner signaling/branding.

RawLockTime.description: Transaction locktime. Values below 500,000,000 are interpreted as block heights; values at or above are Unix timestamps.

txid.description: Transaction ID of the output being spent

vout.description: Output index being spent (u16: coinbase is 65535, mempool.space uses u32: 4294967295)

prevout.description: Information about the previous output being spent

witness.description: Witness data (stack items, present for SegWit inputs; hex-encoded on the wire)

is_coinbase.description: Whether this input is a coinbase (block reward) input

inner_redeemscript_asm.description: Inner redeemscript in assembly (for P2SH-wrapped SegWit: scriptsig + witness both present)

inner_witnessscript_asm.description: Inner witnessscript in assembly (for P2WSH: last witness item decoded as script)

Vout.description: Index of the output being spent in the previous transaction

value.description: Value of the output in satoshis

Witness.description:
Transaction witness: a stack of byte arrays, one per witness item.

Wraps `bitcoin::Witness` (single-buffer layout with offsets, much
more compact than `Vec<Vec<u8>>`). Serializes as a JSON array of
hex strings - the format used by Bitcoin Core REST and mempool.space
and matching brk's `script_sig: ScriptBuf` (bytes internally, hex
on the wire).

Weight.description: Weight in weight units (WU). Max block weight is 4,000,000 WU.

SigOps.description:
BIP-141 sigop cost. The block-level budget is 80,000, so a `u32`
fits a single tx's count with room to spare.

Witness sigops count as 1; legacy and P2SH-redeem sigops count as 4.
Five vbytes per sigop is the policy adjustment Core applies in
`nSigOpCost` to discourage sigop-heavy txs (`max(weight/4, sigops*5)`).

AddrAfterTxidParam.description: Bitcoin address + last-seen txid path parameters (Esplora-style pagination)

after_txid.description: Last txid from the previous page (return transactions strictly older than this)

address.description: Bitcoin address to validate (can be any string)

isscript.description: Whether this is a script address (P2SH)

iswitness.description: Whether this is a witness address

witness_version.description: Witness version (0 for P2WPKH/P2WSH, 1 for P2TR)

error_locations.description: Error locations (empty array for most errors)

merkle_root.description: Merkle root of the transaction tree

mediantime.description: Median time of the last 11 blocks

BlockInfoV1.description: Block information with extras, matching mempool.space /api/v1/blocks

merkle_root.description: Merkle root of the transaction tree

mediantime.description: Median time of the last 11 blocks

stale.description: Whether this block has been replaced by a longer chain

BlockExtras.description: Extended block data matching mempool.space /api/v1/blocks extras

reward.description: Total block reward (subsidy + fees) in satoshis

pool.description: Mining pool that mined this block

avgFee.description: Average fee per transaction in satoshis

coinbaseRaw.description: Raw coinbase transaction scriptsig as hex

coinbaseSignature.description: Coinbase output script in ASM format

feePercentiles.description: Fee amount percentiles in satoshis: [min, 10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 90%, max]

segwitTotalSize.description: Total size of segwit transactions in bytes

utxoSetChange.description:
UTXO set change (total outputs - total inputs, includes unspendable like OP_RETURN).
Note: intentionally differs from utxo_set_size diff which excludes unspendable outputs.
Matches mempool.space/bitcoin-cli behavior.

utxoSetSize.description: Total spendable UTXO set size at this height (excludes OP_RETURN and other unspendable outputs)

firstSeen.description: Timestamp when the block was first seen (always null, not yet supported)

BlockPool.description: Mining pool identification for a block

minerNames.description: Miner name tags found in coinbase scriptsig

Hex.description:
Hex-encoded string. Transparent wrapper over `String`: serializes
as a plain JSON string and derefs to `str`, so anywhere `&str` or
`AsRef<[u8]>` is expected the `Hex` "just works".

BlockTimestamp.description: Block information returned for timestamp queries

BlockStatus.description: Block status indicating whether block is in the best chain

in_best_chain.description: Whether this block is in the best chain

height.description: Block height (only if in best chain)

next_best.description: Hash of the next block in the best chain (null if tip)

BlockHashTxIndex.description: Block hash + transaction index path parameters

BlockTxIndex.description:
Position of a transaction within a single block (0 = coinbase).
Distinct from `TxIndex`, which is the chain-wide global tx index.

BlockHashStartIndex.description: Block hash + starting transaction index path parameters

start_index.description: Starting transaction index within the block (0-based)

PoolInfo.description: Basic pool information for listing all pools

TimePeriod.description:
Time period for mining statistics.

Used to specify the lookback window for pool statistics, hashrate calculations,
and other time-based mining series.

PoolsSummary.description: Mining pools response for a time period

pools.description: List of pools sorted by block count descending

blockCount.description: Total blocks in the time period

lastEstimatedHashrate3d.description: Estimated network hashrate over last 3 days (H/s)

lastEstimatedHashrate1w.description: Estimated network hashrate over last 1 week (H/s)

PoolStats.description: Mining pool with block statistics for a time period

blockCount.description: Number of blocks mined in the time period

rank.description: Pool ranking by block count (1 = most blocks)

PoolDetail.description: Detailed pool information with statistics across time periods

blockCount.description: Block counts for different time periods

blockShare.description: Pool's share of total blocks for different time periods

estimatedHashrate.description: Estimated hashrate based on blocks mined (H/s)

totalReward.description: Total reward earned by this pool (sats, all time; None for minor pools)

PoolBlockCounts.description: Block counts for different time periods

PoolBlockShares.description: Pool's share of total blocks for different time periods

24h.description: Share of blocks in last 24 hours (0.0 - 1.0)

1w.description: Share of blocks in last week (0.0 - 1.0)

PoolHashrateEntry.description: A single pool hashrate data point.

share.description: Pool's share of total network hashrate (0.0 - 1.0)

PoolSlugAndHeightParam.description: Mining pool slug + block height path parameters

HashrateSummary.description: Summary of network hashrate and difficulty data.

DifficultyEntry.description: A single difficulty data point in the hashrate summary.

time.description: Unix timestamp of the difficulty adjustment

DifficultyAdjustmentEntry.description:
A single difficulty adjustment entry.
Serializes as array: [timestamp, height, difficulty, change_percent]

block_count.description: Number of recent blocks to include

RewardStats.description: Block reward statistics over a range of blocks

totalReward.description: Total coinbase rewards (subsidy + fees) in sats

BlockFeesEntry.description: A single block fees data point.

avgHeight.description: Average block height in this window

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp at the window midpoint

avgFees.description: Average fees per block in this window (sats)

BlockRewardsEntry.description: A single block rewards data point.

avgHeight.description: Average block height in this window

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp at the window midpoint

avgRewards.description: Average coinbase reward per block (subsidy + fees, sats)

BlockFeeRatesEntry.description: A single block fee rates data point with percentiles.

avgHeight.description: Average block height in this window

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp at the window midpoint

BlockSizesWeights.description: Combined block sizes and weights response.

BlockSizeEntry.description: A single block size data point.

avgHeight.description: Average block height in this window

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp at the window midpoint

BlockWeightEntry.description: A single block weight data point.

avgHeight.description: Average block height in this window

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp at the window midpoint

MempoolBlock.description: Block info in a mempool.space like format for fee estimation.

blockSize.description: Total serialized block size in bytes (witness + non-witness).

blockVSize.description: Total block virtual size in vbytes

nTx.description: Number of transactions in the projected block

fastestFee.description: Fee rate for fastest confirmation (next block)

halfHourFee.description: Fee rate for confirmation within ~30 minutes (3 blocks)

hourFee.description: Fee rate for confirmation within ~1 hour (6 blocks)

MempoolInfo.description: Mempool statistics with incrementally maintained fee histogram.

count.description: Number of transactions in the mempool

vsize.description: Total virtual size of all transactions in the mempool (vbytes)

total_fee.description: Total fees of all transactions in the mempool (satoshis)

fee_histogram.description: Fee histogram: `[[fee_rate, vsize], ...]` sorted by descending fee rate

VSize.description: Virtual size in vbytes (weight / 4, rounded up). Max block vsize is ~1,000,000 vB.

NextBlockHash.description:
Content hash of the projected next block (block 0 of the mempool
snapshot). Same value as the mempool ETag. Opaque token: pass back
as `since` on `/api/v1/mempool/block-template/diff/{hash}` to fetch
deltas.

MempoolRecentTx.description: Simplified mempool transaction for the `/api/mempool/recent` endpoint.

ReplacementNode.description:
One node in an RBF replacement tree. The node's `tx` replaced each
entry in `replaces`, recursively.

time.description:
First-seen timestamp, duplicated here to match mempool.space's
on-the-wire shape.

fullRbf.description: Any predecessor in this subtree was non-signaling.

interval.description:
Seconds between this node's `time` and the successor that
replaced it. Omitted on the root of an RBF response.

mined.description:
`Some(true)` iff this node's tx is currently confirmed. Absent
on serialization otherwise.

RbfTx.description:
Transaction summary carried inside an RBF replacement node. Shape
matches mempool.space's `/api/v1/tx/:txid/rbf` and
`/api/v1/replacements` responses.

rbf.description: BIP-125 signaling: at least one input has sequence < 0xffffffff-1.

fullRbf.description:
Only populated on the root `tx` of an RBF response. `true` iff
this tx displaced at least one non-signaling predecessor.

BlockTemplate.description:
Projected next-block contents from Bitcoin Core's `getblocktemplate`
(block 0 of the snapshot). Returned by
`GET /api/v1/mempool/block-template`.

hash.description:
Pass back as `<hash>` on
`/api/v1/mempool/block-template/diff/{hash}` to fetch deltas.

stats.description: Aggregate stats for this block (size, vsize, fee range, ...).

BlockTemplateDiff.description:
Delta between the current `getblocktemplate` projection and a prior
one identified by `since`. Returned by
`GET /api/v1/mempool/block-template/diff/{hash}`.

`order` carries the full new template in template order: each entry
is either a `Retained(idx)` pointing into the prior template (which
the client cached at `since`) or a `New(tx)` inline body. Walk it
once to rebuild the new template; no separate `added` array to
cross-reference.

`removed` is redundant (computable from `order` by collecting prior
indices that don't appear) but shipped for cache-eviction ergonomics.

hash.description: Current next-block hash. Use as `since` on the next diff call.

since.description: Echoed prior hash the diff was computed against.

order.description:
New template in order. Each entry is either an index into the
prior template's transactions or a full transaction body.

removed.description:
Txids that left the projected next block since `since`
(confirmed, evicted, replaced, or pushed past block 0).

BlockTemplateDiffEntry.description:
One slot of the new template in a `BlockTemplateDiff`.

Untagged on the wire so JSON type disambiguates the variants:
- `Retained(idx)` serializes as a bare integer - index into the
  transactions of the prior template (which the client cached at
  `since`).
- `New(tx)` serializes as a transaction object - a body that was
  not in the prior template and must be added at this position.

Reconstruction is a single pass: for each entry, either copy
`prior[idx]` or append the inline body.

CpfpInfo.description: CPFP (Child Pays For Parent) information for a transaction.

ancestors.description: Ancestor transactions in the CPFP chain.

bestDescendant.description: Best (highest fee rate) descendant, if any.

descendants.description: Descendant transactions in the CPFP chain.

effectiveFeePerVsize.description:
Effective fee rate considering CPFP relationships (sat/vB).
This is the seed's chunk feerate after lift-merging, i.e. the
rate Core/mempool.space would surface for this tx.

sigops.description:
BIP-141 sigop cost for the seed tx (witness sigops count as 1,
legacy and P2SH-redeem sigops count as 4).

vsize.description: Virtual size of the seed tx (vbytes).

cluster.description:
Cluster the seed belongs to: full tx list, SFL-linearized chunks,
and the seed's chunk index. Omitted when the seed has no
ancestors and no descendants (matches mempool.space).

CpfpEntry.description: A transaction in a CPFP relationship.

CpfpCluster.description:
CPFP cluster: the connected component the seed belongs to, plus its
SFL linearization.

txs.description: All txs in the cluster, in topological order (parents before children).

chunks.description: SFL-emitted chunks ordered by descending feerate.

chunkIndex.description: Index into `chunks` of the chunk containing the seed tx.

CpfpClusterTxIndex.description:
Position of a transaction inside a `CpfpCluster.txs` array. Cluster-local,
has no meaning outside the enclosing cluster.

CpfpClusterChunk.description:
One SFL chunk inside a `CpfpCluster`. `txs` is in topological order
(matches `CpfpCluster.txs` ordering); the chunk's `feerate` is the
per-chunk SFL feerate and is the same for every tx in this chunk.

RbfResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /api/v1/tx/:txid/rbf`. Both fields are null
when the tx has no known RBF history within the mempool monitor's
graveyard retention window.

MerkleProof.description: Merkle inclusion proof for a transaction

TxOutspend.description: Status of an output indicating whether it has been spent

spent.description: Whether the output has been spent

txid.description: Transaction ID of the spending transaction (only present if spent)

vin.description: Input index in the spending transaction (only present if spent)

status.description: Status of the spending transaction (only present if spent)

Vin.description: Input index in the spending transaction

TxidsParam.description:
Query parameter for transaction-times endpoint.

Extracted manually because `serde_urlencoded` (and serde derive in general)
doesn't support repeated keys like `txId[]=a&txId[]=b`. The schema is still
declared via `JsonSchema` so the OpenAPI spec lists the parameter and the
generated client SDKs see `txids: List[Txid]`.

txId[].description: Transaction IDs to look up (max 250 per request).

tags.name: Server
tags.description: API metadata, health monitoring, and OpenAPI specifications.

tags.name: Series
tags.description:
Access thousands of Bitcoin network time-series data. Query historical statistics across various indexes (date, week, month, block height) with JSON or CSV output.

**Note:** Series names are subject to change while the project is in active development.

tags.name: General
tags.description:
General Bitcoin network information including difficulty adjustments and price data.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: Addresses
tags.description:
Query Bitcoin address data including balances, transaction history, and UTXOs. Supports all address types: P2PKH, P2SH, P2WPKH, P2WSH, and P2TR.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: Blocks
tags.description:
Retrieve block data by hash or height. Access block headers, transaction lists, and raw block bytes.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: Mining
tags.description:
Mining statistics including pool distribution, hashrate, difficulty adjustments, block rewards, and fee rates across configurable time periods.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: Fees
tags.description:
Fee estimation and projected mempool blocks.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: Mempool
tags.description:
Monitor unconfirmed transactions. Get mempool statistics, transaction IDs, fee histogram, and recent transactions.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: Transactions
tags.description:
Retrieve transaction data by txid. Access full transaction details, confirmation status, raw hex, merkle proofs, and output spend information.

*[Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/docs/api/rest) compatible.*

tags.name: URPD
tags.description:
UTXO Realized Price Distribution. For each (cohort, date) pair, supply is grouped by the close price at which each UTXO was last moved. One snapshot is emitted per UTC day.

Each bucket carries `supply` (BTC), `realized_cap` (USD, = `price_floor * supply`), and `unrealized_pnl` (USD, = `(close - price_floor) * supply`, can be negative).

Aggregate with the `agg` query parameter (alias `bucket`):
- `raw`: one bucket per rounded price (default).
- `lin200` / `lin500` / `lin1000`: linear buckets, $200 / $500 / $1000 wide.
- `log10` / `log50` / `log100` / `log200`: logarithmic buckets, N bins per price decade.

Discovery flow: `GET /api/urpd` (cohorts), `GET /api/urpd/{cohort}` (latest), `GET /api/urpd/{cohort}/dates` (history), `GET /api/urpd/{cohort}/{date}` (specific).
